replication_study.hypothesis: Mean Aversiveness (0–6 scale) for Covid Verbal items is greater than for NonCovid Verbal items, controlling for crossed random effects (participant, item, and geographic unit).

data_plan.dataset_identifier: Bischetti Survey US replication sample (Jan 2021) — Bischetti_Survey_Part1_deidentify.csv + Bischetti_Survey_Part2_deidentify.csv
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single wave, early 2021 (see Analysis_updated.Rmd timestamps and narrative). Items span pic1–pic216 across two survey parts.
data_plan.sample_size: Current files show unique participant_id counts: Part 1 N=1336; Part 2 N=1051 (exact merged N post-cleaning to be determined by code; Analysis_updated.Rmd notes ~1000 recruited, with partials retained).
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Individual humor-item rating provided by a participant
data_plan.access_details: CSV files are included locally under /app/data. No special credentials needed.

qualification.explanation: This dataset is appropriate because it reproduces the same measurement paradigm as the original study (item-level ratings of funniness and aversiveness on a 7-point scale, plus demographics and personality scales), but with a different participant population (primarily US-based, per “state” values and Prolific recruitment in the code). This offers an independent sample to assess robustness of the original claim.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Original paper measured funniness and aversiveness on 7-point scales and compared humor types (Covid Verbal, NonCovid Verbal, Meme, Strip) using linear mixed-effects models with crossed random effects (original_paper.pdf; post_registration.json: method/models/outcomes). The replication data contain the same rating variables (e.g., picX_disturbing and picX_funny columns; Bischetti_Survey_Part1_deidentify.csv and Bischetti_Survey_Part2_deidentify.csv) and the same auxiliary constructs (CHS and LOT-R items; see dataset columns and Analysis_updated.Rmd). The Analysis_updated.Rmd reproduces the original lme4 approach (lmer, emmeans) and the same factor structure once the item-to-category label is merged.
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original data were collected in Italy during March 18–30, 2020 with province-level geography (original_paper.pdf; post_registration.json data.wave_or_subset). The replication dataset was collected later (Jan 2021 per Analysis_updated.Rmd), uses US state codes for geography (e.g., assigned 'CO' in Analysis_updated.Rmd), and relies on Prolific recruitment (Analysis.Rmd/Analysis_updated.Rmd references to PROLIFIC_PID and de-identified participant_id). Also, the replication code drops the age random slope due to convergence issues (Analysis_updated.Rmd), which is a minor modeling deviation from the full original random-effects structure. Finally, the critical item-to-category mapping (Method .xlsx files) is not present in replication_data and must be supplied as a CSV key to create the ‘label’ factor.

data_plan.notes: Critical dependency missing: an item-to-category mapping for pic IDs to {covid-verbal, covid-meme, covid-strip, non-verbal}. The provided Bichetti_DataDictionary.csv is a variable dictionary, not the mapping. Without this key, we cannot form the ‘label’ factor needed to test the focal aversiveness contrast. Additional notes: Aversiveness raw data are on 1–7; Analysis_updated.Rmd rescales to 0–6 (Aversiveness - 1). Age is recorded as year of birth and is converted to age as 2021 - yob in Analysis_updated.Rmd. Paths in the Rmd files currently use relative locations (../Data, ../Method) that must be rewritten to /app/data. The code also includes explicit lists of duplicate session IDs to drop (Analysis_updated.Rmd).

steps: Import Part 1 and Part 2 de-identified CSVs from /app/data.
Apply the duplicate session removals listed in Analysis_updated.Rmd.
Merge datasets by participant_id (retain all rows; partial completions are allowed as in Analysis_updated.Rmd).

Reshape to long format: keep participant_id, state, age (year of birth), and all *_disturbing columns. Melt into columns: name (derived from original column names: replace 'disturbing' with 'anote') and Aversiveness.

Merge the item-to-category mapping CSV (to be provided) to assign each item to a category label in {covid-verbal, covid-meme, covid-strip, non-verbal}. If labels use 'covid-text'/'non-text', recode to 'covid-verbal'/'non-verbal'.
Recode label as a factor with covid-verbal as the reference level.
Recode Aversiveness to 0–6 by subtracting 1, matching Analysis_updated.Rmd and original scaling.
Convert age from year of birth to numeric age (2021 - yob), as in Analysis_updated.Rmd.

Fit a linear mixed-effects model for Aversiveness with fixed effect label and crossed random intercepts: participant_id, item (name), and geographic unit (state). Include the numeric contrast structure used in Analysis_updated.Rmd to enable participant-level random slopes if convergence permits; otherwise use the simplified model documented there.

Use emmeans to estimate marginal means for each label and explicitly test the contrast: NonCovid Verbal vs Covid Verbal. Extract estimate (difference in points on 0–6), t-statistic, p-value, and 95% CI.
As a robustness check (optional, secondary), refit including additional fixed effects (e.g., CHS/LOTR) if desired and compare the focal contrast.
planned_method.models: Primary: Linear mixed-effects model (lmer) for Aversiveness ~ label + random effects (participant_id, item/name, state). Attempt participant-level random slopes for label components as in Analysis_updated.Rmd; if non-convergent, use the simplified random-effects structure documented there.
planned_method.outcome_variable: Aversiveness rating per item (0–6 scale after recoding).
planned_method.independent_variables: Humor type label with levels {covid-verbal (reference), covid-meme, covid-strip, non-verbal}.
planned_method.control_variables: Random intercepts for participant_id, item (name), and state. Optional secondary analyses may add fixed effects for CHS, LOT-R, age, and gender to mirror original modeling.
planned_method.tools_software: R (lme4, lmerTest, emmeans, rio, reshape, optimx, openssl).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Adjusted marginal mean difference in Aversiveness between Covid Verbal and NonCovid Verbal items (points on 0–6 scale).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: t-test on the emmeans contrast (Satterthwaite approximation via lmerTest).

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion at the row level for missing Aversiveness or missing mapping/label. State missingness is permitted but rows with missing state will be dropped by lmer if state is in the random-effects structure.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Single focal contrast (no correction). If secondary funniness analyses are added, label them exploratory without multiple-testing corrections.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Two-tailed alpha=0.05. Support for replication if the contrast is positive and statistically significant; report estimate, 95% CI, t, and p.

files.Analysis.Rmd: Legacy demonstration analysis using example CSVs and PROLIFIC_PID as participant key; reads mapping from ../Method/*.xlsx and builds a full random-effects model including age random slopes.
files.Analysis_updated.Rmd: De-identified pipeline using participant_id, with duplicate session removal, merging, long-format transformation, item label merge (expects ../Method/*.xlsx), Aversiveness recoding to 0–6, and an updated, simplified lmer model due to convergence limits. Computes emmeans for label.
files.Bischetti_Survey_Part1_deidentify.csv: Part 1 responses: item ratings (picX_disturbing and picX_funny), demographics (age as year of birth; gender; state), CHS (coping_*), LOT-R (lot_*), and participant_id.
files.Bischetti_Survey_Part2_deidentify.csv: Part 2 responses: complementary set of item ratings (picX_disturbing and picX_funny) and participant_id.
files.Bischetti_DataDictionary.csv: Variable-level metadata (variable names, descriptions, allowed values); does not include the item-to-category mapping needed for analysis.

codebase.notes: Known issues to resolve before execution: (1) Provide an item-to-category mapping CSV (e.g., /app/data/item_key.csv) with columns {name or pic_id, label in {covid-verbal, covid-meme, covid-strip, non-verbal}}. This replaces the missing ../Method/*.xlsx files. (2) Update all file paths in Analysis_updated.Rmd from ../Data/... to /app/data/... and from ../Method/... to /app/data/.... (3) Ensure lme4, lmerTest, emmeans, rio, reshape, optimx, and openssl are installed. (4) If the full random-slope structure fails to converge, use the simplified model shown in Analysis_updated.Rmd.

instructions: Place all input CSVs under /app/data (Part1/Part2 de-identified CSVs, and the new /app/data/item_key.csv mapping).

Edit Analysis_updated.Rmd to: (a) import from /app/data/Bischetti_Survey_Part1_deidentify.csv and /app/data/Bischetti_Survey_Part2_deidentify.csv; (b) import mapping from /app/data/item_key.csv; (c) write any outputs to /app/data.
Extract the emmeans contrast for NonCovid Verbal vs Covid Verbal from the model summary to obtain the estimate (points on 0–6), t-statistic, p-value, and 95% CI.
comparison_metrics: Sign and magnitude of the aversiveness difference (NonCovid Verbal vs Covid Verbal). Target direction: Covid Verbal > NonCovid Verbal (i.e., difference > 0).
Magnitude benchmark: original estimate ~ +0.39 points on a 7-point scale (reported as 0–6 after recoding), with t=+2.26, p<.05 (initial_details.txt; post_registration.json).
Confidence interval overlap and practical equivalence assessment (report 95% CI).
